mnemonics.title: List vs Set vs Queue
mnemonics.content: L-S-Q: Lists store indexes, Sets store uniqueness, Queues store sequences. Think: 'List is a store with index shelves, Set is a unique collector, Queue is a waiting line'.

mnemonics.title: Hash vs Tree vs Linked
mnemonics.content: Hash = Fast (hash table), Tree = Sorted (Red-Black), Linked = Ordered (insertion order). Remember: 'He T Li' - Hash, Tree, Linked cover most needs.

mnemonics.title: Iterator Methods
mnemonics.content: Remember I.H.N.R: Iterator hasNext(), next(), remove(). For ListIterator: A.S.N.P.R.B - add(), set(), next(), previous(), remove(), hasPrevious().

mnemonics.title: Map Operations
mnemonics.content: PUT returns old value, REMOVE returns value removed, CONTAINS returns boolean. Remember: 'PReRCP' - Put returns old, Remove returns, Contains boolean.

acronyms.usage: Stack follows LIFO principle - think of a stack of plates
Queue follows FIFO - think of people in a line
HashMap/ getput, ArrayList random access - instant regardless of size
LinkedList random access, iterating all elements - grows with size
TreeSet, TreeMap - halves search space each step
Collection interfaces define ADTs like List, Set, Queue

visualTips.visualization: ArrayList is like a row of numbered lockers (fixed positions), LinkedList is like a treasure hunt (each item points to the next)
Think of a hash table as filing cabinets with numbered drawers - hash function decides which drawer (bucket), then search within drawer for exact match
A balanced binary search tree like a family hierarchy - each node has at most 2 children, left smaller, right larger
Iterator is like a cursor on a train track - hasNext() checks if train can move, next() actually moves and brings you to the next station

analogies.analogy: Think of Collection Framework like a kitchen utensil set - different tools (ArrayList, HashSet, HashMap) for different cooking jobs, all following the same 'utensil' interface pattern.
Like a growing city - when population (elements) exceeds 75% of capacity (buildings), the city expands (doubles) and everyone gets new addresses (rehashing).
Comparable is like your nationality (one natural identity). Comparator is like hiring a translator for a specific meeting (can have many for different purposes).

analogies.analody: Fail-fast iterators are like security cameras - they immediately 'alarm' (throw exception) if they detect the collection being modified while iterating, except through their own remove() method.
